title: Wildcard Pattern Matching
description:
Implement wildcard matching with support for '?' and '*' where '?' matches any single character and '*' matches any sequence (including empty). Return True if the pattern matches the full string.

    

Constraints:
    - 0 ≤ |s|,|p| ≤ 2000
explanation:
Intuition
dp[i][j] is whether s[:i] matches p[:j]. If p[j-1] is '*', dp[i][j] is OR of dp[i][j-1] (use '*' as empty) and dp[i-1][j] (consume one char). If p[j-1] is '?' or equals s[i-1], inherit diagonal.

Approach
1. Initial Setup: Initialize dp[0][0]=True; first row depends on consecutive '*' in p
2. Main Algorithm: Fill table by cases for '*', '?', or exact match
3. Return Result: Return dp[n][m]

Complexity Analysis
- Time Complexity: O(nm)
- Space Complexity: O(nm)

Key Insights
- Compress leading '*' in the pattern row
- Star can represent empty or many characters

Common Pitfalls
- Mixing roles of i and j breaks indices
- Forgetting dp[i][j-1] path for empty star
hints: Initialize first row for leading stars
